action.reasoning: Observation: Iteration 5's constant-resolution ResNet achieved a new best rmse_z500=1018.6 with only 5 epochs and no residual connection, but validation loss bottomed at epoch 2 (0.1168) then rose, indicating early overfitting. Analysis: The architecture is clearly superior to the U-Net, but it needs stronger regularization, longer training, and a persistence residual (delta prediction) to generalize better. Also, since the primary metric is rmse_z500, up-weighting Z500 in the loss by 2x should help. Using mixed-precision (AMP) will cut training time by ~30-40%, letting me safely run 30 epochs within the 2.86h budget (~75-90 min training + eval). Decision: Update train.py with residual=True, channel-specific loss weights [2,1], dropout=0.1, weight_decay=5e-4, ReduceLROnPlateau scheduler, gradient clipping, and AMP. Run 30 epochs with patience=12. Keep model.py and predict.py unchanged.
